Container.Name: icloudpd
Container.Overview:
An Alpine Linux Docker container for ndbroadbent's iCloud Photos Downloader. I use it for syncing the photo streams of all the iDevices in my house back to my server because it's the only way of backing up multiple devices to a single location. It uses the system keyring to securely store credentials, has HEIC to JPG conversion capability, and can send Telegram, Prowl, Pushover, WebHook, DingTalk, Discord, openhab, IYUU and WeCom notifications.
It is recommended to use the keyring to securely store credentials. Please connect to the container and run `sync-icloud.sh --Initialise` command manually to start the process of saving your password to the keyring and Apple sends a confirmation code via text or 2FA code to your device which needs to be entered into the console. You can also run this command from the unraid console to start the authentication process. `docker exec -it icloudpd sync-icloud.sh --Initialise`
Create a `.mounted` file inside `iCloud` folder in the photos directory for the container to start syncing photos. This file MUST be created manually and sync will not start without it.

Config.Name: Apple ID
Config.Description: Apple ID used to access iCloud.

Config.Name: Time Zone
Config.Description: Sets container timezone. Example: Europe/Amsterdam

Config.Name: Config Path
Config.Description: Stores configuration, cookies, and keyring.

Config.Name: Download Path
Config.Description: Where downloaded photos and videos will be stored. A .mounted file must exist inside this folder before syncing will start.

Config.Name: Download Interval
Config.Description: Seconds between downloads. Valid values: 21600 (6h), 43200 (12h), 86400 (24h), 129600 (36h), 172800 (48h), 604800 (7d). Defaults to 86400 if an invalid value is set.

Config.Name: Download Delay
Config.Description: Delay first download in minutes (0-60). Use to stagger multiple containers.

Config.Name: Folder Structure
Config.Description: Folder structure for downloaded files. Use 'none' for a flat structure. Default: {:%Y/%m/%d}

Config.Name: Albums With Dates
Config.Description: Organize albums into subfolders by date (uses folder_structure). Default: false

Config.Name: Libraries With Dates
Config.Description: Organize libraries into subfolders by date (uses folder_structure). Default: false

Config.Name: Authentication Type
Config.Description: Type of authentication on your iCloud account. Use 'MFA' or 'Web' (if no MFA). Default: MFA

Config.Name: Notification Days
Config.Description: Notify n days before MFA cookie expires. A single notification is sent per day. Default: 7

Config.Name: Single Pass
Config.Description: Set to true to exit after a single sync instead of looping. If used, set container restart policy to 'no'.

Config.Name: Skip Check
Config.Description: Skip the check for new files. Recommended for libraries with more than a few thousand photos. Default: false

Config.Name: Photo Size
Config.Description: Size to download: original, medium, thumb, adjusted, alternative. Can be comma-separated for multiple sizes. Default: original

Config.Name: Skip Live Photos
Config.Description: Skip downloading Live Photos. Default: false

Config.Name: Live Photo Size
Config.Description: Live photo size: original, medium, or thumb. Ignored if skip_live_photos is true. Default: original

Config.Name: Skip Videos
Config.Description: Skip downloading videos. Default: false

Config.Name: Recent Only
Config.Description: Only download this number of recently added photos. Leave empty to download all.

Config.Name: Until Found
Config.Description: Download recent photos until n previously downloaded consecutive photos are found. Leave empty to download all.

Config.Name: Photo Album
Config.Description: Download only from specific album(s). Comma-separated. Use 'all albums' to download all albums.

Config.Name: Skip Album
Config.Description: Skip specific albums when using photo_album. Example: All Photos,Videos,Screenshots

Config.Name: Photo Library
Config.Description: Download only from specific shared library/libraries. Comma-separated. Use 'all libraries' to download all.

Config.Name: Skip Library
Config.Description: Skip specific libraries when using photo_library. Example: PrimarySync

Config.Name: Align RAW
Config.Description: For assets with RAW and JPEG, treat RAW in specified size: original, alternative, or as-is. Default: as-is

Config.Name: File Match Policy
Config.Description: Policy to identify and de-duplicate files. Options: name-size-dedup-with-suffix, name-id7. Default: name-size-dedup-with-suffix

Config.Name: Convert HEIC to JPEG
Config.Description: Convert downloaded HEIC files to JPEG, retaining the original. Default: false

Config.Name: JPEG Quality
Config.Description: JPEG conversion quality (0-100). Default: 90

Config.Name: JPEG Path
Config.Description: Custom path for converted JPEGs. Defaults to download path if not set.

Config.Name: Auto Delete
Config.Description: Scan 'Recently Deleted' folder and delete those files locally. Default: false

Config.Name: Delete After Download
Config.Description: Move file to Recently Deleted after download. Cannot be combined with auto_delete. Default: false

Config.Name: Delete Accompanying Files
Config.Description: Delete generated JPG and MOV files that accompany HEICs (live photo MOVs, converted JPEGs). Default: false

Config.Name: Delete Empty Directories
Config.Description: Delete empty directories after sync. Only runs if folder_structure is not 'none'. Default: false

Config.Name: Set Exif DateTime
Config.Description: Write DateTimeOriginal EXIF tag from file creation date. Warning: alters local file and causes re-download with dedup suffix. Default: false

Config.Name: Keep Unicode
Config.Description: Keep unicode characters in file names. Set to false to strip non-ASCII chars. Default: false

Config.Name: Live Photo MOV Filename Policy
Config.Description: Filename policy for Live Photo MOV files: suffix (adds _HEVC) or original (same name as photo). Default: suffix

Config.Name: Sideways Copy Videos
Config.Description: Copy or move downloaded videos to a separate directory (video_path). Default: false

Config.Name: Sideways Copy Videos Mode
Config.Description: How to handle sideways video copy: 'copy' (leave original) or 'move' (requires delete_after_download=true). Default: copy

Config.Name: Video Path
Config.Description: Destination directory for sideways video copy/move. Required when sideways_copy_videos is true.

Config.Name: Nextcloud Upload
Config.Description: Enable upload to Nextcloud after download. Default: false

Config.Name: Nextcloud URL
Config.Description: Nextcloud server URL. Example: https://my.server.local/nextcloud/

Config.Name: Nextcloud Username
Config.Description: Username of the Nextcloud account to upload to.

Config.Name: Nextcloud Password
Config.Description: Password for the Nextcloud account.

Config.Name: Nextcloud Target Dir
Config.Description: Root folder in Nextcloud to place files in. Cannot be the root of the server.

Config.Name: Nextcloud Delete
Config.Description: Delete file from Nextcloud when removed locally. Requires auto_delete=true. Default: false

Config.Name: Notification Type
Config.Description: Notification provider to use. Options: Prowl, Pushover, Telegram, Webhook, openhab, Dingtalk, Discord, IYUU, WeCom, Gotify, Bark, msmtp, signal. Leave empty to disable.

Config.Name: Notification Title
Config.Description: Title used in notifications. Default: boredazfcuk/iCloudPD

Config.Name: Download Notifications
Config.Description: Send notifications with a summary after file downloads. Default: true

Config.Name: Delete Notifications
Config.Description: Send notifications with a summary after file deletions. Default: true

Config.Name: Startup Notification
Config.Description: Send a notification when the container starts. Default: true

Config.Name: Silent File Notifications
Config.Description: Send low-priority (silent) notifications for file downloads and deletions. Default: false

Config.Name: msmtp Host
Config.Description: [msmtp] SMTP server hostname.

Config.Name: msmtp Port
Config.Description: [msmtp] SMTP server port. Normally 465 or 587. Default: 587

Config.Name: msmtp Username
Config.Description: [msmtp] SMTP login username.

Config.Name: msmtp Password
Config.Description: [msmtp] SMTP login password.

Config.Name: msmtp From Address
Config.Description: [msmtp] Sender email address.

Config.Name: msmtp TLS
Config.Description: [msmtp] Enable TLS encryption: on or off. Default: on

Config.Name: msmtp Auth
Config.Description: [msmtp] Enable SMTP authentication: on or off. Default: on

Config.Name: msmtp Extra Args
Config.Description: [msmtp] Optional extra arguments for msmtp. Example: --tls-starttls=off

Config.Name: Gotify Server URL
Config.Description: [Gotify] Server hostname. Example: server.domain.tld (no port, no protocol). Use gotify_https to toggle HTTPS.

Config.Name: Gotify App Token
Config.Description: [Gotify] Application token for Gotify.

Config.Name: Gotify HTTPS
Config.Description: [Gotify] Use HTTPS for Gotify server URL. Default: false (HTTP)

Config.Name: Gotify Priority
Config.Description: [Gotify] Message priority (0-10). Default: 0

Config.Name: Signal Host
Config.Description: [Signal] Hostname of the bbernhard/signal-cli-rest-api container.

Config.Name: Signal Port
Config.Description: [Signal] Port the signal-cli-rest-api container listens on.

Config.Name: Signal Number
Config.Description: [Signal] Phone number of the Signal device to send from.

Config.Name: Signal Recipient
Config.Description: [Signal] Recipient phone number for Signal messages.

Config.Name: Bark URL
Config.Description: [Bark] Full Bark push URL. For the official server use: api.day.app

Config.Name: Pushover Token
Config.Description: [Pushover] Application API token.

Config.Name: Pushover User Key
Config.Description: [Pushover] Pushover user key.

Config.Name: Pushover Priority
Config.Description: [Pushover] Priority: -1, 0, 1, or 2. Default: 0

Config.Name: Pushover Retry
Config.Description: [Pushover] Retry interval in seconds (priority 2 only). Default: 60

Config.Name: Pushover Expire
Config.Description: [Pushover] Notification expiration in seconds (priority 2 only). Default: 3600

Config.Name: Pushover Sound
Config.Description: [Pushover] Custom notification sound. See https://pushover.net/api#sounds for values.

Config.Name: Healthchecks URL
Config.Description: [Healthchecks] Full ping URL for Healthchecks.io.

Config.Name: Webhook Server
Config.Description: [Webhook/openhab] Hostname of the webhook server.

Config.Name: Webhook Port
Config.Description: [Webhook/openhab] Port for the webhook server. Default: 8123

Config.Name: Webhook Path
Config.Description: [Webhook/openhab] Path on the webhook server. Must start and end with /. Default: /api/webhook/

Config.Name: Webhook ID
Config.Description: [Webhook/openhab] Webhook ID. For openhab use 'state'.

Config.Name: Webhook HTTPS
Config.Description: [Webhook/openhab] Use HTTPS. Default: false (HTTP)

Config.Name: Webhook Body
Config.Description: [Webhook] Body key for the webhook request. HA=data, Discord=content, IFTTT=value1. Default: data

Config.Name: Webhook Insecure
Config.Description: [Webhook] Allow insecure HTTPS certificates (e.g. self-signed). Default: false

Config.Name: Telegram Token
Config.Description: [Telegram] Bot token from BotFather.

Config.Name: Telegram Chat ID
Config.Description: [Telegram] Chat ID for the bot. Prefix with - for group chats.

Config.Name: Telegram Polling
Config.Description: [Telegram] Enable polling to allow remote sync/auth via chat messages. Default: false

Config.Name: Telegram Silent Notifications
Config.Description: [Telegram] Send file download notifications silently. Default: false

Config.Name: Telegram Server
Config.Description: [Telegram] Proxy server FQDN if Telegram is blocked in your country.

Config.Name: Telegram HTTP
Config.Description: [Telegram] Use HTTP instead of HTTPS if Telegram is restricted to HTTP only. Default: false

Config.Name: Prowl API Key
Config.Description: [Prowl] API key from the Prowl website.

Config.Name: DingTalk Token
Config.Description: [DingTalk] Access token from DingTalk app Security Settings.

Config.Name: Discord ID
Config.Description: [Discord] First part of the Discord webhook URL (numbers only).

Config.Name: Discord Token
Config.Description: [Discord] Second part of the Discord webhook URL.

Config.Name: IYUU Token
Config.Description: [IYUU] Access token for IYUU notifications.

Config.Name: Initialise Keyring
Config.Description: Run password/keyring setup and create MFA cookie on next start.

Config.Name: Convert All HEICs
Config.Description: Convert HEICs that do not have a corresponding JPEG.

Config.Name: Force Convert All HEICs
Config.Description: Convert all HEICs regardless of existing JPEG presence. 2-minute delay before running.

Config.Name: Force Convert All HEICs in /mnt
Config.Description: Convert all HEICs in /mnt path. 2-minute delay before running.

Config.Name: Remove All JPGs
Config.Description: Remove all JPEGs that have a matching HEIC source file.

Config.Name: Correct JPEG Time Stamps
Config.Description: Correct JPEG timestamps to match their accompanying HEIC files.

Config.Name: Remove Keyring
Config.Description: Delete stored password and keyring data. Use when changing Apple ID password.

Config.Name: Enable Debugging
Config.Description: Enable verbose debug output.

Config.Name: Disable Debugging
Config.Description: Disable debug logging.

Config.Name: Upload Library to Nextcloud
Config.Description: Force upload entire local library to Nextcloud.

Config.Name: Sideways Copy All Videos
Config.Description: Copy or move all videos in download path to video_path.

Config.Name: List Albums
Config.Description: List all albums available in the iCloud account.

Config.Name: List Libraries
Config.Description: List all shared libraries available in the iCloud account.

Config.Name: User
Config.Description: Container user name (e.g. 'nobody'). Also used as the Telegram remote-sync trigger word.

Config.Name: User ID (PUID)
Config.Description: User ID to match permissions on host.

Config.Name: Group
Config.Description: Group name for file permissions.

Config.Name: Group ID (PGID)
Config.Description: Group ID to match host group.

Config.Name: Force GID
Config.Description: Allow creation of a group with a pre-existing group ID. Use with caution.

Config.Name: Directory Permissions
Config.Description: Permissions to set on directories in the download destination. Default: 750

Config.Name: File Permissions
Config.Description: Permissions to set on downloaded files. Default: 640
